blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: I have a new binder system.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: Color-coded by subject. It's actually really satisfying.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I'll take your word for it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'll get it the weekend before.
Savannah made a face that communicated something complex about preparedness and love, but let it go.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I have last year's notebooks still.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: Kit, you can't use last year's notebooks.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: There's still blank pages in them.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: You two are genuinely the least prepared people I know and somehow you always survive the year.
He meant it as a joke but it landed somewhere slightly more true than that, and he looked at his burger instead of at her.
